description_long: This is a hand tool with a long, slender, dark metal shaft and a robust, ergonomically contoured handle. The handle is predominantly brown with a textured, dark gray grip section, likely designed for improved handling and slip resistance. The shaft appears to be made of steel, extending from the handle and terminating in a cross-shaped tip, characteristic of a Phillips screwdriver. The handle's surface shows a combination of smooth and grippy textures, with visible seams and slight color variations suggesting molded plastic or rubberized material. There are no visible brand markings or logos on the handle or shaft. The tool appears well-used, with some discoloration and minor surface wear, especially on the handle.
description: A Phillips screwdriver with a brown, contoured handle featuring a textured dark gray grip and a long, dark metal shaft. The tool shows minor wear and no visible branding.
description_view_0: The screwdriver is seen at an angle highlighting the brown handle and the full length of the dark shaft, with the Phillips tip partially visible.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the textured grip section of the handle and the straight alignment of the shaft, with the handle's ergonomic contours more apparent.
description_view_2: A direct end-on view of the handle, showing the circular cross-section and the shaft extending outward, with the Phillips tip facing away.
description_view_3: The screwdriver is viewed from the side, displaying the full length of the shaft and the handle's grip texture, with the tip pointing left.
attribute_handle_type: contoured, plastic with textured rubberized grip
attribute_visible_wear_or_damage: minor discoloration and surface wear on handle
